---
title: "Open Educational Resources: Accessible, transparent, and reusable learning objects"
format: html
---



|  |  |  |  |
|------------------|------------------|------------------|------------------|
| [![](images/clipboard-972878262.png)](Introduction%20to%20OERs/Intro-Learning%20Outcomes.html) | [![](images/clipboard-2783689849.png)](OERs%20for%20users/User-learning%20outcomes.html) | [![](images/clipboard-3520176154.png)](OERs%20for%20creators/Creator-learning%20outcomes.html) | [![](images/clipboard-560624670.png)](Modular%20OERs/Modular-learning%20outcomes.html) |
| Introduction to OERs | OERs for Users | OERs for Creators | Advanced: Modular OERs Using R and Quarto |

### Audience

This programme is designed for:

-   Educators, instructional designers, and learning technologists

-   Library and research support staff

-   Students and lifelong learners

-   Anyone wishing to create, adapt, reuse, or understand Open Educational Resources (OERs)

### Programme-wide Learning Outcomes

By the end of all four units, learners will be able to:

-   Define OERs and their attributes using internationally recognised definitions (UNESCO, Creative Commons).

-   Navigate, evaluate, and reuse OERs appropriately.

-   Create OERs that apply the {{< meta glossary.fiveRs >}}, {{< meta glossary.FAIR >}}, and good licensing practice.

-   Produce reusable and computationally reproducible OERs using </abbr>, {{< meta glossary.R >}} with {{< meta glossary.Quarto >}}, including structuring source files, managing local terminology, and enabling remixing.

-   Share OERs in accessible, editable, future proof formats with transparent licensing and version control.
